paragraphs.body: When my family first dropped me off at college, I initially felt a great sense of freedom. The first few hours, I was excited to be on my own without the restrictions that accompanied living with my parents. However, it did not take long for all of that bliss to quickly morph into anxiety, as I realized I was now on my own hours from where I have lived my whole life, and hours away from everyone that I loved. As time went on, my small dorm room began to feel like a prison. The walls seemed to close in more and more everyday as I felt completely trapped. I did not know anybody here, or anywhere to go for comfort. The most difficult part of all was being away from my main source of comfort; Rocco. My boyfriend Rocco and I had spent every day together in the summer, and years together previous to that. Not being able to see him was overwhelming, and created a feeling of entrapment in Pittsburgh as I felt like I was being kept away from him. We had little to no time to communicate over the phone and as an eighteen-year-old at college for the first time, I had no way out and no way to get to him. Being trapped was a familiar feeling, as I had felt like I was back to being a child, entrapped in my room when I was grounded by my parents.

annotations.text: Being trapped was a familiar feeling, as I had felt like I was back to being a child, entrapped in my room when I was grounded by my parents.

paragraphs.body: When I was nine, being grounded also felt like being in a prison within my own room. The pillow where I would lay my head to rest every night felt like a dog’s cage. The smell of must from being stuck in my room for an extensive amount of time began to fill my nostrils and overwhelm me. My light pink and green walls became dull as I began to dread looking at something I usually love. My bed that was as comfy as a cloud now felt like the top of a garbage dump from laying on it for hours. My room was so silent at this point I could hear the clock slowly ticking, the fan spinning, and the faint sounds of my sister and her friend’s laughter outside. I was trapped in here for what had now felt like years, but it’s only been 5 minutes. This punishment had become my worst nightmare.

annotations.text: My room was so silent at this point I could hear the clock slowly ticking, the fan spinning, and the faint sounds of my sister and her friend’s laughter

annotations.context_left: The pillow where I would lay my head to rest every night felt like a dog’s cage. The smell of

paragraphs.body: Similar to this, Pittsburgh had begun to feel like an entrapment with no way out, as my relationship with Rocco now felt distant and difficult where it used to bring me comfort. Being away from Rocco felt similar to my entrapment that my parents would place me in. This feeling never seemed to fade away, as the constant anxiety of feeling stuck in place constantly weighed on me. The burden of waking up and repeating the same schedule in the same, miserable place daily was brutal, and I wondered how I would ever break out of this pattern. However, my grandparents coming to visit Pittsburgh was when I could finally open my eyes and realize that this ‘prison’ was all internal. When walking across a bridge with them, I came across a lock with the initials R+C scribbled on it. Initially, my heart warmed seeing Rocco and I’s initials written on the lock. While the lock may seem small and insignificant, it seemed to open my eyes and make me aware of my current situation. I started to realize that just because Rocco wasn’t physically in front of me, there were signs of love all around, as this little lock posed as a sign that just because I was away from home does not mean that I am lost. I still was extremely lucky to have Rocco’s support as I began this new journey.

annotations.text: came across a lock with the initials R+C scribbled on it.

Similar to this, Pittsburgh had begun to feel like an entrapment with no way out, as my relationship with Rocco now felt distant and difficult where it used to bring me comfort. Being away from Rocco felt similar to my entrapment that my parents would place me in. This feeling never seemed to fade away, as the constant anxiety of feeling stuck in place constantly weighed on me.

paragraphs.body: With this, I began trying to change my behavior and feel less miserable about being here, and in turn, learn from this experience. I was emerging into adulthood, on my own for the first time. I realized that this was all a part of my growth, and being away from Rocco was teaching me how to exist on my own. Finally, I felt lighter, as if this realization had completely shifted the scenery of Pittsburgh from me. My room began to seem cozier, I began enjoying the people here, and I enjoyed the time I was able to spend time on the phone with Rocco instead just longing to be with him in person. As the feeling of isolation began to dwindle, I was reminded of my childhood once again, as growing up started to show me the point of being grounded.

annotations.text: As the feeling of isolation began to dwindle, I was reminded of my childhood once again, as growing up started to show me the point of being grounded.
annotations.comment: I don't quite get the sentence, but the idea seems clear and important here.

paragraphs.body: I used to think that my parents just hated me, and that is why they would place me into my room for hours on end. However, as I look back at those times, I can remember that each time I can out of the room finally, I felt like I had learned something. I would not repeat the behavior that initially got me into my room. With this, I began growing and maturing. Even though I dreaded being locked in my room, I always felt a sense of freedom and changing upon being released. With this, I was able to mature and grow up once and for all. That miserable time spent in my room taught me lessons that I would not have learned any other way, and as I would lay in bed in my college dorm that used to feel like a prison, I finally realized that it was the same thing as my childhood bedroom; a prison that brought me a beautiful destination.

annotations.text: finally realized that it was the same thing as my childhood bedroom; a prison that brought me a beautiful destination.

paragraphs.body: Now looking back on this all I see it as a place of growth for me. It was all a necessary journey that in the moment was difficult, but now I got something amazing out of it. My childhood room which once seemed like a cage, helped me to developed the ability to see being grounded wasn’t a punishment similarly how my college dorm which once seemed like a prison keeping me from Rocco now has turned my eye to be a place where I discover my own strengths and attributes. As I now navigate my way through this awakening chapter I realize that change and feeling trapped isn’t a punishment but a sign that good things are to come. The lessons I learned from my childhood have helped me and shaped me into the person I am today, the exact way I hope what I have overcome will help me overcome harder parts of life such as getting a job, or even getting married. It’s funny how something as small as a little lock with our initials on it in a big city could bring me such clarity in a unfamiliar place. In the end this “prison” of being trapped at college was truly never about actually being stuck, it was about learning to set myself free from the entrapment mindset I had in my head. This overall mindset shifting lesson has taught me much about myself, and also allowed me to recognize all that I have learned from Rocco as I have now adapted to this new chapter on my own, taking what I’ve been shown all throughout my life and using it to be who I am today.
